task.prompt: Write a function `censor_terms(text, terms)` that censors banned terms in text.

tests.code: assert censor_terms('Nothing to hide', []) == 'Nothing to hide'

gold_spec.goal: Replace each banned term occurrence with asterisks while respecting literal term boundaries.

inputs.name: text
inputs.description: text that may contain banned terms

outputs.name: censored
outputs.description: text with matching banned terms replaced by asterisks

postconditions: Match terms case-insensitively but preserve all non-matching text exactly.
Treat every term as literal text, not as a regular expression pattern.
A match is valid only when the character before the match, if any, is not an ASCII letter, digit, or underscore.
A match is valid only when the character after the match, if any, is not an ASCII letter, digit, or underscore.
Hyphen and punctuation are boundaries; underscore is not a boundary.
If multiple terms could match at the same position, use the longest term.
Replace each matched character with one asterisk, preserving the original matched length.
edge_cases: Do not censor a term inside a larger alphanumeric or underscore word.
Terms may contain spaces or punctuation.
An empty terms list returns the original text unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: All and only boundary-valid literal term occurrences are replaced with same-length asterisk runs.

known_weakness.description: The raw prompt invites naive substring replacement and misses literal matching, longest-match, case-insensitivity, and ASCII boundary rules.
known_weakness.naive_spec_gap: missing substring boundary, regex escaping, underscore, and overlap semantics
